IHO Definition: A generic term for an administrative region within a country at a level below that of the sovereign state.
IHO Definition: The name of a nation.
IHO Definition: Known in various countries as a postcode, or ZIP code, the postal code is a series of letters and/or digits that identifies each postal delivery area.
IHO Definition: Named aggregations between two or more aids to navigation and/or navigationally relevant features.

li.p: IHO Definition: -
IHO Definition: -
IHO Definition: -

td.p: IHO Definition: A non-formatted digital text string.
Remarks: Should be used, for example, to hold the information that is for short cautionary or explanatory notes.
Therefore, text populated in text must not exceed 300 characters. Text may be in English, or in a national language.
No formatting of text is possible within text. If formatted text, or text strings exceeding 300 characters, is required, then an alternate concept should be used.
IHO Definition: The method of human communication, either spoken or written, consisting of the use of words in a structured and conventional way.
Remarks: The language is encoded by a 3 character code following ISO 639-2/T.
IHO Definition: Words set at the head of a passage or page to introduce or categorize.
IHO Definition: The file name of an externally referenced text file.
IHO Definition: The location of a fragment of text or other information in a support file.
Remarks: Application schemas must describe how the associated file is identified. The associated file will commonly be named in a file reference co-attribute of the same complex attribute.
Each DCEG must specify requirements for the format of the associated file and the semantics of file locator.
For example, the value of file locator may be an HTML ID in an HTML file, line number in a text file, or a bookmark in a PDF file.

IHO Definition: The factor to be applied to the variable component of an uncertainty equation so as to provide the best estimate of the variable horizontal or vertical accuracy component for positions, depths, heights, vertical distances and vertical clearances.
IHO Definition: The best estimate of the fixed horizontal or vertical accuracy component for positions, depths, heights, vertical distances and vertical clearances.
IHO Definition: Textual information in national language characters.
Remarks: Encodes any textual information about an object using a specified national language. The textual information could be, for example, a list, a table or a text.
This should be used, for example, to hold the information that is shown on paper charts by cautionary and explanatory notes.
IHO Definition: The best estimate of the horizontal accuracy of horizontal clearances and distances.
Remarks: The error is assumed to be positive and negative. The plus/minus character must not be encoded.
IHO Definition: The best estimate of the accuracy of a bearing.
IHO Definition: An assessment of the likelihood of change over time.

li.p: IHO Definition: Indication of the possible impact of a significant event (for example hurricane, earthquake, volcanic eruption, landslide, etc), which is considered likely to have changed the seafloor or landscape significantly.

p.strong: Likely to Change and Significant Shoaling Expected

li.p: IHO Definition: Continuous or frequent change (for example river siltation, sand waves, seasonal storms, icebergs, etc) that is likely to result in new significant shoaling.

p.strong: Likely to Change But Significant Shoaling Not Expected

li.p: IHO Definition: Continuous or frequent change (for example sand wave shift, seasonal storms, icebergs, etc) that is not likely to result in new significant shoaling.
IHO Definition: Continuous or frequent change to non-bathymetric features (for example river siltation, glacier creep/recession, sand dunes, buoys, marine farms, etc).
IHO Definition: Significant change to the seafloor is not expected.
IHO Definition: Not having been assessed.

td.p: IHO Definition: The value considered by the Data Producer to be the maximum (largest) scale at which the data is to be displayed before it can be considered to be ‘grossly overscaled’.
IHO Definition: The smallest intended viewing scale for the data.
IHO Definition: Different stages of the establishment of aids to navigation.
IHO Definition: Different stages of removal or temporary removal of aids to navigation.
IHO Definition: Different stages of replacing aids to navigation.
IHO Definition: Different types of changes in fixed aids to navigation.
IHO Definition: Different types of changes in floating aids to navigation.
IHO Definition: Changes related to the operational status of audible and fog signals.
IHO Definition: Changes related to the operational status of lighted aids to navigation.

p.strong: Front leading/range light Light range reduced
Rear leading/range light Light range reduced
Front leading/range light is operating properly
Rear leading/range light is operating properly
Front leading/range beacon restored to normal
Rear leading/range beacon restored to normal

td.p: IHO Definition: Direction or superscription of a letter, package, etc., specifying the name of the place to which it is directed, and optionally a contact person or organisation who should receive it.

li.p: Delivery Point (see )
City Name (see )
Administrative Division (see )
Country Name (see )
Postal Code (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: A directional light is a light illuminating a sector of very narrow angle and intended to mark a direction to follow.

li.p: Moire Effect (see )
Orientation (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: Provides the name of an entity, defines the national language of the name, and provides the option to display the name at various system display settings.

li.p: Display Name (see )
Language (see )
Name (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: An active period of a single fixed event or occurrence, as the date range between discrete start and end dates.

li.p: Date End (see )
Date Start (see )

td.p: Remarks: Dates must be encoded in the format YYYYMMDD; using 4 digits for the calendar year (YYYY) and, optionally, 2 digits for the month (MM) (for example April = 04) and 2 digits for the day (DD). When no specific month and/or day is required/known, the values are replaced with dashes (-). The date range of a recurring event or occurrence must be encoded using periodicDateRange.
IHO Definition: A sector is the part of a circle between two straight lines drawn from the centre to the circumference.

li.p: Colour (see )
Directional Character (see )
Light Visibility (see )
Sector Limit (see )
Value of Nominal Range (see )
Sector Information (see )
Sector Extension (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The number of features of identical character that exist as a colocated group.

li.p: Multiplicity Known (see )
Number of Features (see )
The angular distance measured from true north to the major axis of the feature.

In ECDIS, the mode in which information on the ECDIS is being presented. Typical modes include: north-up — as shown on a nautical chart, north is at the top of the display; Ships head-up — based on the actual heading of the ship, (e.g. Ships gyrocompass); course-up display — based on the course or route being taken.
Orientation Uncertainty (see )
Orientation Value (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The active period of a recurring event or occurrence.

li.p: Date End (see )
Date Start (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The distance between two successive peaks (or other points of identical phase) on an electromagnetic wave in the radar band of the electromagnetic spectrum.

li.p: Radar Band (see )
Wave Length Value (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The sequence of times occupied by intervals of light/sound and eclipse/silence for all light characteristics or sound signals.

li.p: Light Characteristic (see )
Signal Group (see )
Signal Period (see )
Signal Sequence (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: Describes the characteristics of a light sector.

li.p: Light Characteristic (see )
Light Sector (see )
Signal Group (see )
Signal Period (see )
Signal Sequence (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: Additional textual information about a light sector.

li.p: Language (see )
Text (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: A sector is the part of a circle between two straight lines drawn from the centre to the circumference. The sector limit specifies the limits of the sector In a clockwise direction around the central feature (for example a light).

li.p: Sector Limit One (see )
Sector Limit Two (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: A sector is the part of a circle between two straight lines drawn from the centre to the circumference. Sector limit one specifies the first limit of the sector. The order of sector limit one and sector limit two is clockwise around the central feature (for example a light).

li.p: Sector Bearing (see )
Sector Line Length (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: A sector is the part of a circle between two straight lines drawn from the centre to the circumference. Sector limit two specifies the second limit of the sector. The order of sector limit one and sector limit two is clockwise around the central feature (for example a light).

li.p: Sector Bearing (see )
Sector Line Length (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: Textual information about the shape of a non-standard topmark.

li.p: Language (see )
Text (see )

td.p: Remarks: No formatting of text is possible within shape information. If formatted text is required, then an associated text file referenced by the complex attribute textual description must be used.
IHO Definition: The sequence of times occupied by intervals of light and eclipse for all light characteristics.

li.p: Signal Duration (see )
Signal Status (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: Provides an indication of the vertical and horizontal positional uncertainty of bathymetric data, optionally within a specified date range.

li.p: Fixed Date Range (see )
Horizontal Position Uncertainty (see )
Vertical Uncertainty (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The dimensions of a cable to give its length and diameter.

li.p: Cable Length (see )
Height Length Units (see )
Diameter (see )
Aton Commissioning (see )
Aton Removal (see )
Aton Replacement (see )
Fixed Aton Change (see )
Floating Aton Change (see )
Audible Signal Aton Change (see )
Lighted Aton Change (see )
Electronic Aton Change (see )
Sector Limit (see )
Sector Information (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The dimensions of a sinker/anchor to give its three dimensional shape measurements.

li.p: Height Length Units (see )
Horizontal Length (see )
Horizontal Width (see )
Vertical Length (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: A description of the method used to obtain a position.(proposed by CCG)

li.p: Positioning Equipment (see )
NMEAString (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The best estimate of the accuracy of a position.

li.p: Uncertainty Fixed (see )
Uncertainty Variable Factor (see )

td.p: Remarks: The expected input is the maximum of the two-dimensional error. The error is assumed to be positive and negative.
IHO Definition: Textual information about the feature. The information may be provided as a string of text or as a file name of a single external text file that contains the text.

li.p: File Locator (see )
File Reference (see )
Headline (see )
Language (see )
Text (see )

td.p: Remarks: At least one of the sub-attributes file reference or text must be populated.The sub-attribute file reference is generally used for long text strings or those that require formatting, however, there is no restriction on the type of text (except for lexical level) that can be held in files referenced by sub-attribute file reference.

IHO Definition: Encodes the file name of a single external text file that contains the text in a defined language, which provides additional textual information that cannot be provided using other allowable attributes for the feature.

li.p: File Reference (see )
Language (see )

td.p: IHO Definition: The best estimate of the vertical accuracy of depths, heights, vertical distances and vertical clearances.

li.p: Uncertainty Fixed (see )
Uncertainty Variable Factor (see )

td.p: Remarks: Encodes the vertical uncertainty associated with any vertical measurement.
